title: Ptilotrichum purpureum
uses: Ptilotrichum purpureum, commonly known as the red-stemmed bush, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin infections. In traditional medicine, it is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. It is a small, shrubby plant with purple flowers and is often found in dry, rocky areas.
